title: capability-runtime-lease-v1
description: Ephemeral live endpoint lease for a running capability. Producer: a running tool or service wrapper that wants to advertise its live endpoint. Consumer: Spore (reads leases when resolving a capability id to a preferred live endpoint). A lease is considered stale when expires_at_unix has passed or the pid no longer exists.

capability_id.description: The capability this lease satisfies, e.g. 'memory.store.v1'. One lease per capability; a tool advertising multiple capabilities writes multiple lease files.

transport.description: Active transport kind for the live endpoint.

pid.description: Process id of the serving process. Spore uses this to validate that the lease is still live.

leased_at_unix.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when this lease was written.

expires_at_unix.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when this lease expires. Null means the lease is open-ended and Spore should only invalidate by pid liveness. Set a finite value for short-lived endpoints.

endpoint.description: For 'unix-socket' transport: absolute socket path. For 'tcp' transport: 'host:port' string. Null or absent for 'stdio' and 'cli' transport.

command.description: For 'cli' or 'stdio' transport: the command or binary path to invoke. Null or absent when a socket endpoint is used.

version.description: Running version of the tool, if available. Used to detect version mismatch between a running service and the installed registry.

status.description: Self-reported health at lease time. A tool sets 'degraded' if it is running but partially unavailable.

message.description: Optional human-readable detail about the health state.
